mnemonics: chmod for change mode, who for who, done for done
u for user, g for group, o for owner
keyPoints: Relative permissions: - (for owner), + (for group), space (for others) are appended to the file's current permissions.
Absolute permissions: 0-7 (for owner), 8-15 (for group), 16-23 (for others) represent the permissions for each access (read, write, execute).
Using the absolute method, each digit in the octal number represents the permissions for the user, group, and others respectively (rwx).
